nodes.context: Singaporean-origin organized crime syndicate that controlled the European heroin trade from the 1970s to 1990s, based in Amsterdam and Bangkok.
Penang-based criminal organization with strong ties to Ah Kong.
Singaporean gang from which Ah Kong originated; means 'buddies' or 'a group of close friends'.
Rival gang of See Tong in Singapore.
Hong Kong-based triad involved in drug trade in Amsterdam; rival of Ah Kong.
Hong Kong triad operating in Rotterdam; had a dispute with Ah Kong.
Hong Kong triad mentioned as established in Amsterdam.
Gang established by Johnny in Bangkok after splitting from Ah Kong.
Rotterdam-based gang that replaced Ah Kong's influence after 1997.
Singapore-originated organized crime syndicate controlling European heroin trade in 1970s-1990s, based in Amsterdam and Bangkok.
Penang-based criminal organization with strong ties to Ah Kong.
Triad group whose boss Ngau Si clashed with Ah Kong.
Singaporean-Malaysian gang led by Cha Boh Hai, based in Rotherham, clashed with Big Circle Gang.
Chinese gang that clashed with Sin Ma.
Italian organized crime group with business dealings with Ah Kong.
Balkan organized crime group with business dealings with Ah Kong.
Organised crime and drugs syndicate that controlled European heroin trade from 1970s to 1990s, based in Amsterdam and Bangkok.
Penang-based criminal organization with ties to Ah Kong.
Biker gang whose Danish branch held parties at Roland Tan's restaurant.

edges.evidence_quote: over the years they had built strong ties with the infamous Penang -based Sio Sam Ong
edges.context: Ah Kong built strong ties with Sio Sam Ong.

edges.evidence_quote: Roland Tan and his brethren... founded Ah Kong but operated under the See Tong flag.
edges.context: Ah Kong was founded by former See Tong members and initially operated under the See Tong flag.

edges.evidence_quote: Roland Tan... and about ten of his fellow gang members from See Tong had armed themselves with machetes to attack two members of their rival gang, Pek Kim Leng
edges.context: See Tong attacked members of rival gang Pek Kim Leng, resulting in a death.
Ah Kong and 14K are listed as rivals in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: two members of Ah Kong... went to settle a dispute with the Wo Shing Wo triad in Rotterdam
edges.context: Ah Kong members had a dispute with Wo Shing Wo, resulting in violence.

edges.evidence_quote: Johnny went to Bangkok and established the Hainan gang.
edges.context: After splitting from Ah Kong, Johnny founded the Hainan gang as a separate entity.

edges.evidence_quote: they began to lose their influence to the Sin Ma gang based in Rotterdam
edges.context: Ah Kong lost influence to the Sin Ma gang after 1997.

edges.evidence_quote: over the years they had built strong ties with the infamous Penang-based Sio Sam Ong.
edges.context: Ah Kong built strong ties with Sio Sam Ong.

edges.evidence_quote: There was an occasion when Siam-kia was disrespected by the 14K boss known as Ngau Si. A clash between the two gangs erupted. Ah Kong defeated 14K
edges.context: Ah Kong clashed with and defeated 14K after a disrespect incident.

edges.evidence_quote: He had business dealings with the Italian mafia
edges.context: Ah Kong leader Dennis had business dealings with the Italian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: He had business dealings with the Italian mafia and Yugoslavian crime syndicate
edges.context: Ah Kong leader Dennis had business dealings with the Yugoslavian crime syndicate.

edges.evidence_quote: They often clashed with the Tai Hun Zai (Big Circle Gang) from China.
edges.context: Sin Ma clashed with Tai Hun Zai.

edges.evidence_quote: some people from the inner-circle discredited his claim and some said that the leader of the Sin Ma gang - Cha Boh Hai, was the one behind the hit.
edges.context: Sin Ma leader Cha Boh Hai was suspected of ordering the hit on Ah Kong leader Dennis.

edges.evidence_quote: over the years they had built strong ties with the infamous Penang-based Sio Sam Ong
edges.context: Ah Kong built strong ties with Sio Sam Ong.
